properties.titulo_evento: US strikes Iranian sites after Iran launches drones, in latest Gulf flare-up
properties.descricao: U.S. forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites on Saturday after shooting down drones launched by Iran toward the Strait of Hormuz, the U.S. military said.

properties.titulo_evento: The retaking of Cuba
properties.descricao: Decades-old property claims, frozen since the 1960s, are being turned into live instruments of reconquest.

properties.titulo_evento: Smoke rises in illegal Israeli settlement after Iranian missile attack
properties.descricao: Smoke was seen in an illegal Israeli settlement in the occupied West Bank after Iran launched launched missiles.

properties.titulo_evento: Video: Israel claims strike on Iranian petrochemical complex
properties.descricao: Video shows plumes of smoke rising from Mahshahr Petrochemical Complex in Iran’s Khuzestan Province on Monday.

properties.titulo_evento: Israel kills 14 people in attacks across Gaza
properties.descricao: Israel killed at least 14 people in Gaza on Sunday in strikes across Khan Younis, Gaza City, and Deir el-Balah.

properties.titulo_evento: Israel’s strikes on Iran expose Trump’s failure to restrain Netanyahu
properties.descricao: Israel’s strikes on Iran expose Trump’s failure to restrain Netanyahu.

properties.titulo_evento: Asia’s stock markets dive amid Iran-Israel conflict, Wall Street jitters
properties.descricao: South Korea’s main index plunges nearly 9 percent, while stocks in Japan, Taiwan and Hong Kong also see sharp falls.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine, Russia trade fire as Zelenskyy allies back call for direct talks
properties.descricao: UK, France, and Germany support Zelenskyy's proposal for a meeting with Putin, as drone attacks continue.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran releases footage of missiles launched at Israel
properties.descricao: Iran said its attacks were "the beginning of a full week of continuous strikes."

properties.titulo_evento: Knicks' long-awaited NBA run electrifies NYC, with Trump set to attend key game
properties.descricao: The Knicks' stunning winning streak has energised the city as the New York basketball team prepares for the third game of the finals series at its home arena.

properties.titulo_evento: Stock markets fall on tech plunge and renewed Middle East attacks
properties.descricao: Markets in Europe and Asia were hit by a tech sell-off, and oil rises as Iran and Israel launch attacks on each other.

properties.titulo_evento: Zelensky's close European allies set out five conditions for peace talks
properties.descricao: The Ukrainian president's meeting with his staunchest European allies came as US President Donald Trump's focus shifts to the war with Iran.

properties.titulo_evento: Peru election result close as vote counting continues
properties.descricao: The race between right-wing Keiko Fujimori and left-wing Roberto Sánchez has been dominated by concerns over crime and political instability.

properties.titulo_evento: Friendship or leverage: Why is Xi Jinping in North Korea?
properties.descricao: Beijing is trying to reassert influence over a strategically vital yet deeply unpredictable partner.

properties.titulo_evento: Steve Rosenberg: Russia's economic forum overshadowed by drone attacks on St Petersburg
properties.descricao: The BBC's Russia editor saw Putin's flagship economic event overshadowed by Ukrainian drones attacks.

properties.titulo_evento: Houthis join Iran war fight, threatening Red Sea shipping amid Hormuz closure
properties.descricao: Yemen's Houthis declared a ban on Israeli shipping in the Red Sea and claimed a missile strike on Israel, with the Bab el-Mandeb and Hormuz blockades now simultaneously threatening roughly a third of the world's seaborne oil and gas.

properties.titulo_evento: IRGC threatens regional energy assets as Iran trades strikes with Israel
properties.descricao: An IRGC source warned that any further attacks on Iranian energy infrastructure would trigger strikes on energy assets in the broader region, after Israel struck Iran's Karun petrochemical plant in Khuzestan and the IRGC responded within the hour by ...

properties.titulo_evento: Iranian military's joint command announces 'suspension' of attacks on Israel
properties.descricao: Israel launched airstrikes into central and western Iran early on Monday in response to missiles fired from Tehran, the first such barrage since an April ceasefire took hold in the Middle East war.

properties.titulo_evento: Counting is underway in Armenia’s pivotal vote for the South Caucasus future
properties.descricao: Polls close in Armenian’s decisive election for the South Caucasus country and the region’s future, against the backdrop of a Russia-West clash

properties.titulo_evento: Man opens fire in central Israel killing one person and injuring five others
properties.descricao: Israeli media say the attacker carried out the shootings from inside his car, allowing him to reach several locations within a matter of minutes.

properties.titulo_evento: 'Democratic backsliding': Armenia's 'extreme polarisation, hate speech, stigmatization' mar election
properties.descricao: FRANCE 24's Charles Pellegrin is pleased to welcome Dr Narek Sukiasyan, Senior Policy Researcher at Friedrich Ebert Stiftung Armenia and Adjunct Lecturer at American University of Armenia. He argues that while Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's victor...

properties.titulo_evento: Can Xi bring North Korea closer into Beijing's orbit?
properties.descricao: The Chinese leader's first trip to North Korea in 7 years comes as Pyongyang has moved closer to Russia. Kim Jong Un is likely to seek more access to China's economic power, analysts say.

properties.titulo_evento: China's Xi begins rare North Korea visit
properties.descricao: China's President Xi Jinping has kicked off his first visit to North Korea in seven years. It is also Xi's first international trip this year after hosting key summits with US and Russian leaders.

properties.titulo_evento: Teacher protests in Mexico could spoil the World Cup party
properties.descricao: Just before the opening of the World Cup, host country Mexico faces a political tug-of-war. Striking teachers have occupied the central fan zone and have pledged to continue protesting.

properties.titulo_evento: Peru: Presidential election too close to call
properties.descricao: With over 90% of the votes counted in Peru's runoff presidential election, right-wing conservative candidate Keiko Fujimori and left-wing politician Roberto Sanchez are in a neck-and-neck race.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany news: Arson suspected in large-scale power outage
properties.descricao: Around 10,000 households in the southwestern city of Reutlingen have been affected by the outage, which is reminiscent of recent far-left arson attacks in Berlin. DW has more.

properties.titulo_evento: Middle East updates: Iran declares end to attacks on Israel
properties.descricao: Iran's military said it would halt its operations against Israel, but warned that strikes would resume if Israel attacked Lebanon. The announcement came after Trump called for an end to the fighting. Follow DW for more.

properties.titulo_evento: Reports: Iran uses war as cover for increased executions
properties.descricao: Iran already had one of the highest rates of capital punishment in the world. Now, rights groups say, the country is using the current conflict to carry out even more executions, in an attempt to repress dissent.

properties.titulo_evento: Armenia, once Russia's reliable ally, considers an EU future
properties.descricao: Armenians are set to vote on the country's future geopolitical direction on June 7. The election will be a test of public opinion in the long-standing Russia ally, where the population is increasingly eyeing the EU.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany beat USA before World Cup but injuries cast shadow
properties.descricao: Germany’s final match before their World Cup start ended with a 2-1 win. But a tournament-ending injury to rising star Lennart Karl and doubts about veteran Manuel Neuer mean coach Julian Nagelsmann has work to do.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump tells Iran and Israel to stop ‘shooting’ as ceasefire breaks down
properties.descricao: For the first time since April, Iran and Israel engaged in an exchange of airstrikes between Sunday evening and Monday morning.

properties.titulo_evento: The back-channel bid to go soft on Maduro
properties.descricao: A web of oil interests, a fallen congressmember, a special envoy and a fleet of social-media influencers tried to pull the Trump administration toward a more accommodating posture on Venezuela. It almost worked.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran and Israel exchange airstrikes, putting US-backed ceasefire in jeopardy
properties.descricao: Both sides are disregarding U.S. President Donald Trump's calls for deescalation.

properties.titulo_evento: How Donald Trump helped make Spain’s prime minister a ‘rock star’
properties.descricao: Pedro Sánchez may be embattled at home, but his opposition to the war in Iran has boosted his clout on the rest of the continent.

properties.titulo_evento: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq jump as chip stocks rebound, Iran and Israel exchange strikes
The Iran War is Forcing Energy-Importing Countries to Turn Inward
properties.descricao: The Iran war is pushing countries to prioritize domestic energy in order to protect themselves from volatile oil and natural gas markets.

properties.titulo_evento: Europe Watches Its Economic Recovery Fade Into the Distance
properties.descricao: As the war in Iran persists, signs point to a prolonged period of higher prices and slower growth rather than a quick shock.

properties.titulo_evento: Oil Rises After Hostilities Between Iran and Israel Escalate
properties.descricao: Oil prices jumped and stocks plunged after Iran and Israel exchanged strikes, raising doubts about the future of fragile cease-fire in the Middle East.

properties.titulo_evento: OPEC Plus to Boost Oil Production as Ceasefire in Iran Remains Elusive
properties.descricao: The cartel’s move to increase output by 188,000 barrels per day is largely symbolic, with vast amounts of the world’s oil stranded by the effective shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz.

properties.titulo_evento: Alan Riding, Times Correspondent in Latin America and Paris, Dies at 82
properties.descricao: He was a cosmopolitan observer and interpreter of societies he knew firsthand, whether writing about war in Nicaragua or the history and cultural salons of France.
